refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) explicit baseline recording, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintained baselines/calibration updates. High safety criticality demands explicit safety margin derivation and audit capability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance requirements are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (b) baseline is recorded with timestamp, source, and derivation method; (c) history log captures all updates with reason, timestamp, and approver; (d) traceability chain from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline → history is complete and auditable; (e) safety margins are explicitly calculated and documented.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets (cycle time, energy, availability) from worksite mission context inputs
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance requirements as a traceable baseline with metadata (source, timestamp, derivation method)
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail
CR-NGHE-005.4: Calculate and document explicit safety margins for all performance requirements
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability reporting linking worksite inputs → derived requirements → baselines → history

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized performance analytics engine that ingests worksite mission parameters (terrain, payload, cycle profile) and applies deterministic derivation rules to compute performance targets; stores baseline snapshots in a versioned configuration database with full audit logging; exposes traceability queries via API.

DP-B: Distributed performance derivation where each vehicle computes its own performance targets locally based on mission context, syncs to a central repository, and maintains local + central history logs.
DP-C: Manual performance requirement entry by Site Manager with template-driven guidance; system records entries and maintains change history; no automatic derivation.

DP-D: Hybrid approach: automatic derivation engine for standard mission profiles; manual override capability for non-standard scenarios; centralized baseline and history management with role-based audit trail.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Hybrid approach with automatic derivation engine for standard mission profiles, manual override capability for non-standard scenarios, centralized baseline and history management with role-based audit trail and safety margin calculation.

potential_sources: DP-D couples performance derivation logic to mission context interpretation (worksite inputs) — this is intentional and necessary; no hidden coupling to vehicle control or teleoperation logic.
DP-D couples baseline management to audit/history subsystem — acceptable; audit trail is a cross-cutting concern that should be centralized.
DP-D does not couple to real-time vehicle dynamics; derivation is pre-mission or mission-planning phase, decoupled from runtime control.
coupling_check.notes: DP-D avoids over-coupling by separating derivation logic (standard rules engine) from override logic (manual entry with validation). Audit trail is a shared service, not a tight coupling. Decoupling from vehicle runtime is maintained.

assumptions_risks: Assumption: Worksite mission context is well-defined and available at mission-planning time (not discovered at runtime).
Assumption: Standard mission profiles can be identified and their derivation rules codified; non-standard scenarios are rare.
Risk: If mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, automatic derivation may produce incorrect targets; mitigation: validation rules and manual review gates.
Risk: Override capability could be misused to bypass safety margins; mitigation: role-based access control and audit logging.
Risk: Hybrid approach adds complexity; mitigation: clear decision logic for when to use automatic vs. manual path.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Clarify the decision logic for automatic vs. manual derivation: define which mission profile types trigger automatic derivation and which require manual review. Specify the validation rules that must pass before a derived baseline is accepted. Define the override approval workflow and who has authority to override safety margins.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module owns the performance requirement derivation, baseline management, and history tracking subsystem. It integrates mission context inputs (from Site Manager via Tasking interface), applies derivation rules, records baselines, maintains audit trail, and exposes traceability queries. It does not own vehicle-level performance monitoring (owned by Vehicle module) or safety alert generation (owned by Safety Services module), but it provides performance targets that feed into both.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission context inputs (worksite parameters, payload, cycle profile, operational constraints) from Site Manager or mission planning system. Used to trigger performance requirement derivation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs derived performance targets, baseline metadata, and traceability information to Vehicle and Safety Services modules for mission execution and safety monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety constraints and margin requirements from Safety Services; outputs safety-critical performance targets and audit trail for safety-related requirement changes.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides connectivity for centralized baseline and history repository; enables audit logging and traceability query access across distributed stakeholders (Site Manager, Maintenance, System Engineer).

module_containment_scores.notes: Highest containment: performance derivation, baseline management, and history tracking are core NGHE Solution responsibilities. Interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are all well-defined and external. No diffusion risk; logic is cohesive and localized.

Low containment: Vehicle module owns vehicle-level performance monitoring and execution, not requirement derivation or baseline management. Assigning DP-D to Vehicle would scatter derivation logic across multiple vehicle instances and lose centralized audit trail. High diffusion risk.

Low containment: Safety Services owns safety monitoring and alert generation, not performance requirement derivation. While safety margins are part of DP-D, they should be derived in NGHE Solution and consumed by Safety Services. Assigning to Safety Services would couple safety logic to requirement management. High diffusion risk.
Minimal containment: Teleoperation module owns remote operation interface and control, not performance requirement derivation or baseline management. No logical fit. Very high diffusion risk.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the natural owner of performance requirement derivation, baseline management, and history tracking because: (1) it is the system-level integrator that understands mission context and performance targets across all subsystems; (2) centralized baseline and audit trail are critical for traceability and safety compliance; (3) interfaces to mission planning (Tasking), vehicle execution (Telemetry), and safety monitoring (SafetyAlert) are all external and well-defined; (4) no diffusion of logic across multiple modules.
containment_risks: Risk: If NGHE Solution becomes a monolithic 'god module' with too many responsibilities, future decomposition may be difficult. Mitigation: clearly separate performance derivation (rules engine), baseline management (data store), and history tracking (audit service) as internal sub-components with clean interfaces.

Risk: Centralized baseline repository could become a bottleneck if accessed by many concurrent stakeholders. Mitigation: design for read-heavy access patterns; use caching and eventual consistency where appropriate.

Risk: Safety margin calculation is safety-critical; if housed in NGHE Solution, it must be subject to rigorous verification and validation. Mitigation: safety margin logic should be formally specified, independently reviewed, and traced to safety requirements.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define the internal architecture of NGHE Solution's performance management subsystem: (1) separate the derivation rules engine from the baseline data store; (2) specify the audit logging service and its interface to both; (3) define the traceability query API and its consumers (Site Manager, Maintenance, System Engineer); (4) specify the safety margin calculation algorithm and its verification approach; (5) clarify the role of Teleoperation module in accessing performance targets (read-only via Telemetry interface).

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (hybrid automatic + manual derivation with centralized baseline and audit trail) directly addresses all aspects of refined FR-005: automatic derivation from mission context, baseline recording with traceability, managed history with audit trail, and safety margin calculation. DP-D is neither over-constrained nor under-constrained; it provides sufficient detail to guide IM assignment without prescribing implementation details. Coupling check passes: no hidden dependencies on vehicle runtime or teleoperation logic.

diffusion_risks: Low diffusion risk: DP-D is assigned entirely to NGHE Solution module with clear external interfaces (Tasking in, Telemetry out, SafetyAlert inout, Network Link inout). No logic is scattered across Vehicle, Safety Services, or Teleoperation modules.
Audit trail and history management are centralized in NGHE Solution, avoiding duplication across modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution achieves 0.95 containment score for DP-D. All derivation logic, baseline management, and history tracking are cohesive and localized. External interfaces are well-defined and minimal. No module boundary violations detected.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-005 is feasible: (1) test automatic derivation with known mission profiles and validate outputs against expected performance targets; (2) verify baseline recording by inspecting database records for completeness and metadata; (3) verify history tracking by simulating requirement updates and confirming audit trail captures all changes; (4) verify traceability by following chain from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline → history; (5) verify safety margins by reviewing calculation algorithm and testing with safety-critical scenarios.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns performance requirement derivation, baseline management, and history tracking. Vehicle module consumes performance targets via Telemetry interface. Safety Services module consumes safety-critical targets via SafetyAlert interface. Site Manager provides mission context via Tasking interface. Maintenance and System Engineer access traceability via Network Link. No ambiguity in ownership or responsibility.

recommended_revisions: Refine the decision logic for automatic vs. manual derivation: specify which mission profile types are 'standard' (automatic) and which are 'non-standard' (manual review required). Define the validation rules that must pass before a derived baseline is accepted.

Specify the safety margin calculation algorithm: define how safety margins are derived from mission context, performance targets, and safety constraints. Specify the verification and validation approach for safety-critical calculations.
Define the override approval workflow: specify who has authority to override automatic derivation or safety margins, what justification is required, and how overrides are logged and audited.
Clarify the internal architecture of NGHE Solution's performance management subsystem: separate derivation rules engine, baseline data store, and audit logging service with clean internal interfaces.

Specify the traceability query API: define what queries are supported (e.g., 'show all requirements derived from this mission context', 'show history of changes to this baseline'), who can access them, and what audit logging is required.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 refined is not atomic; it contains five distinct sub-FRs that must be decomposed further to guide detailed design and implementation. The refined FR is well-formed and the selected DP (DP-D) is sound, but the next level of functional decomposition is necessary to allocate responsibility for each sub-FR to specific design parameters and implementation modules. Proceeding to the next decomposition level will clarify the internal architecture of the performance management subsystem and enable detailed design of the derivation rules engine, baseline data store, audit logging service, and traceability query API.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Derive performance targets (cycle time, energy, availability) from worksite mission context inputs
CR-NGHE-005.2: Record derived performance requirements as a traceable baseline with metadata (source, timestamp, derivation method)
CR-NGHE-005.3: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail
CR-NGHE-005.4: Calculate and document explicit safety margins for all performance requirements
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability reporting linking worksite inputs → derived requirements → baselines → history

assumptions: Worksite mission context is well-defined and available at mission-planning time (not discovered at runtime).
Standard mission profiles can be identified and their derivation rules codified; non-standard scenarios are rare.
Performance requirement derivation is a pre-mission or mission-planning phase activity, not a runtime activity.
Centralized baseline and history repository is feasible and acceptable to stakeholders (Site Manager, Maintenance, System Engineer).
Safety margin calculation can be formalized as a deterministic algorithm based on mission context and safety constraints.
Role-based access control is available for managing override authority and audit trail access.
open_risks: If mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, automatic derivation may produce incorrect targets; mitigation: validation rules and manual review gates.
Override capability could be misused to bypass safety margins; mitigation: role-based access control and audit logging.
Hybrid approach adds complexity; mitigation: clear decision logic for when to use automatic vs. manual path.
Centralized baseline repository could become a bottleneck if accessed by many concurrent stakeholders; mitigation: design for read-heavy access patterns.
Safety margin calculation is safety-critical; must be subject to rigorous verification and validation.
Performance requirement derivation rules may need to evolve as mission profiles change; mitigation: design for rule updates without code changes.
evidence_needed: Specification of standard mission profile types and their derivation rules.
Specification of validation rules that must pass before a derived baseline is accepted.
Specification of the safety margin calculation algorithm and its verification approach.
Specification of the override approval workflow and authority matrix.
Design of the internal architecture of NGHE Solution's performance management subsystem.
Design of the traceability query API and its consumers.
Acceptance criteria for performance requirement derivation accuracy (e.g., within X% of actual performance).
Acceptance criteria for audit trail completeness and integrity.
